name: Valentine Candy Heart
recipeInstructions:
Grease an 8-inch heart-shaped cake pan; line the bottom with wax paper. Grease the wax paper.
Sprinkle 1 cup pecans evenly in the pan.
Combine semisweet chocolate and butter in the top of a double boiler; bring water to a boil. Reduce heat to low; cook until chocolate and butter melt, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat.
Place white chocolate in the top of a double boiler; bring water to a boil. Reduce heat to low; cook until white chocolate melts, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat.
Drizzle 1/2 cup semisweet chocolate mixture over pecans. Drizzle 1/2 cup white chocolate over semisweet chocolate.
Chill the mixture in the pan for 15 minutes or until firm.
Set aside 1/2 cup semisweet chocolate mixture. Combine the remaining semisweet chocolate mixture, remaining 3/4 cup pecans, and coconut; stir well. Spread the coconut mixture evenly in the pan over the chilled chocolate. Chill for another 15 minutes or until firm.
Reheat the reserved 1/2 cup semisweet chocolate mixture in the top of a double boiler if necessary. Repeat with the remaining white chocolate if needed. Spoon the reserved semisweet chocolate and reserved white chocolate by tablespoonfuls over the coconut mixture, making sure the chocolates do not overlap. Cut through the melted chocolates with a knife to create a marbled effect. Cover and chill the candy heart for several hours or until firm.
Carefully invert the heart; peel off the wax paper. Invert again onto a serving plate, or if desired, wrap in decorative cellophane. Serve at room temperature; cut the candy into pieces. Yield: 2 pounds.
